chunk_summary: This section outlines strategies for documenting outcome measures and planning relapse prevention in professional nutrition care, emphasizing structured monitoring, step-up criteria, and tailored follow-up plans.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of structured documentation in nutrition care and follow-up monitoring.
Learn how to use specific metrics like BMI-for-age, MUAC, and BP percentiles in pediatric obesity care.
Develop relapse prevention plans tailored to cultural and situational factors.
Apply teach-back methods to ensure caregiver understanding of care plans.
key_facts: BMI-for-age should be used for children aged 2 years and above.
MUAC measurement is recommended for children aged 6–59 months.
Step-up care is advised if centiles continue to cross down after 2–4 weeks of intervention or if systemic symptoms or psychosocial barriers emerge.
Relapse planning should account for predictable setbacks like festivals, weddings, and exam months.
Each visit should end with a one-page prescription including one goal, one measurement, one follow-up date, and an 'urgent return if' list.
reference_quote: Nutrition counselling should be treated like other chronic care: baseline, plan, monitoring, step-up criteria, and relapse management. (NG75)
